reference_standards: BS 8000:11 (Workmanship on building sites - Wall and floor tiling)
City & Guilds 6710 Level 2 Diploma in Wall and Floor Tiling (600/8785/7)
Skills England ST0368 Wall and Floor Tiler Apprenticeship Standard

tasks.title: Tool & Material Identification
tasks.instruction: Lay out 6 tiling tools, photograph them, name each. Select correct adhesive for a given substrate.

checks: Tools laid out on clean, stable surface (not floor edge or unstable area)
No damaged or unsafe tools visible (cracked handles, exposed blades)
Correctly identifies notched trowel (BS 5385 specifies 6mm or 10mm notch depending on tile size)
Correctly identifies manual or electric tile cutter
Correctly identifies spirit level (min 600mm for tiling work per BS 5385)
Correctly identifies tile spacers (appropriate size: 2-3mm for wall, 3-5mm for floor per BS 5385:1)
Correctly identifies grout float
Correctly identifies sponge or wash kit
Selects correct adhesive class for substrate (e.g. C2TE for wall, C2F for floor per BS EN 12004)
All 6 tools correctly named
Correct adhesive-substrate pairing with reasoning
Correct spacer size selected for tile format

tasks.title: Surface Preparation
tasks.instruction: Clean a wall surface, check level, mark horizontal/vertical guidelines with pencil, fix a batten if needed.

checks: PPE worn: safety boots visible, appropriate clothing
Work area clear of trip hazards
Dust/debris managed (brush or vacuum used)
Surface cleaned/brushed before checking level
Spirit level used correctly (held flat against surface, bubble centred checked)
Plumb line or spirit level used to check vertical
Horizontal datum line marked clearly with pencil and level (per BS 5385:1 clause on setting out)
Vertical centre line or starting point marked
Batten fixed horizontally if applicable (level checked, secured with screws not nails for removal)
Surface defects identified (loose plaster, paint, contamination)
Guidelines are visible, straight, and level
Surface is clean and ready to receive adhesive
Batten (if used) is level and secure

tasks.title: Cut a Tile
tasks.instruction: Measure and cut an L-shaped tile to fit around a pipe or obstacle.

checks: Safety glasses/goggles worn during cutting (mandatory per HSE guidance)
Dust mask worn if using electric cutter
Correct PPE for tool type (gloves if handling sharp edges)
Tile cutter used on stable surface
Measurement taken from wall to obstacle accurately (pencil mark on tile)
Measurement transferred correctly to tile (allowing for spacer gap)
Appropriate cutting tool selected for cut type (manual scorer for straight, electric wet cutter for L-shape/curve)
Tile held firmly during cutting
Cut line followed accurately
Edges cleaned/filed if needed (no sharp projections)
Clean cut with no chips or cracks (per BS 5385 workmanship standard)
Tile fits the obstacle with appropriate gap (3-5mm for movement/grouting)
L-shape or curve is accurate to measurement
No visible damage to tile face

tasks.title: Tile a 1m² Section
tasks.instruction: Apply adhesive with notched trowel, place tiles with spacers, check level, clean excess adhesive.

checks: PPE appropriate for adhesive handling
Work area organised (adhesive bucket, tiles, spacers accessible)
No slip/trip hazards on floor
Adhesive mixed to correct consistency (if applicable)
Notched trowel held at ~45° angle to create even ridges (BS 5385:1 requirement)
Adhesive applied to area no larger than can be tiled within open time (~15-20 min for standard adhesive)
Back-buttering used for large format tiles (>300x300mm per BS 5385)
Tiles placed with firm, slight sliding motion to ensure full bed contact
Spacers inserted at all corners consistently
Spirit level used to check flatness across multiple tiles
Tiles adjusted while adhesive is still workable
Excess adhesive cleaned from tile face and joints before setting
Even adhesive coverage (no hollow spots — per BS 5385:1 min 80% bed contact for walls, 95% for wet areas)
Tiles are level with each other (max 1mm lippage per BS 8000:11)
Consistent spacer gaps throughout
Pattern/bond aligned correctly
No adhesive residue on tile faces
Overall section looks professional and even

tasks.title: Grouting & Finishing
tasks.instruction: Mix grout, apply diagonally with float, fill all joints, clean excess with sponge within working time.

checks: Gloves worn (grout is alkaline/irritant)
Knee pads if working on floor
Work area ventilated if using epoxy grout
Grout mixed to correct consistency (firm paste, not runny per manufacturer spec)
Float held at ~45° angle and drawn diagonally across joints (not parallel — to avoid pulling grout out)
All joints filled in one direction first, then cross-direction
Excess grout removed with float edge held at ~90° before it sets
Damp sponge used to clean tile faces within working time (typically 15-30 min)
Sponge rinsed frequently in clean water
Final wipe to remove haze
All joints fully filled — no voids or pinholes (BS 5385 requirement)
Joint depth consistent (grout slightly recessed or flush, not overfilled)
Grout lines straight and even
No grout residue or haze on tile faces
Tiles not scratched during grouting
Overall finish is clean and professional
